meta.title: Water Resources Information System (SIRH) — iCarto
meta.description: Integrated digital platform for water authorities to manage licenses, billing and GIS analysis. Proven in Mozambique and Eswatini.

hero.title: A Single </br>Source of Truth</br>for Water Authorities
hero.description: The integrated GIS-powered platform to manage the full lifecycle of water licenses, professionalise billing and effectively engage with water users.
hero.screenshotAlt: SIRH operation areas map with GIS layers and license overlays

proven.title: Proven at national and regional level
proven.subtitle: 10 years improving water user and licence management

countryList.name: Mozambique
countryList.mapAlt: SIRH map interface showing operation areas in Mozambique

countryList.name: Eswatini
countryList.mapAlt: SIRH map interface showing operation areas in Eswatini

proven.resultsFootnote: Based on implementation experience in Mozambique since 2018 and Eswatini since 2024.

resultsItems.title: Increased number of licenses managed annually

challenges.title: The challenges of fragmented water management
challenges.description: Many water authorities still run licenses and billing on paper ledgers and disconnected spreadsheets. That fragmentation creates daily friction between departments—and makes it harder to enforce legally mandated cost recovery.

items.problem: Licence and billing data scattered across paper files, spreadsheets, and departments without a shared view.
Manual calculations and paper-based processing delay licence renewals and everyday workflows.
Tracking gaps and collection bottlenecks weaken cost recovery and institutional revenue.

capabilities.title: One centralised database</br>for your entire water administration
capabilities.subtitle: Digital capabilities shared across departments, without duplicate files or manual handoffs.

items.title: Standardised Registry
items.description: Unified procedures for registering water users and issuing licenses, aligned with your authority's rules and procedures.
items.imageAlt: SIRH water user and license registry screen

items.title: Georeferenced Mapping
items.description: Register and visualise operation areas, crops, and abstraction or discharge points on interactive maps linked to license records.

items.title: Automated Estimation
items.description: Apply institutional reference values to calculate water consumption automatically—replacing manual estimates in renewals and billing runs.

items.title: Application & Renewal Tracking
items.description: Monitor license applications and renewals through each institutional step, with clear status for staff and supervisors.
items.imageAlt: SIRH license application and renewal workflow

items.title: Document & Report Generation
items.description: Produce official licenses, invoices, and operational reports on demand. Export to PDF, Excel, or geospatial formats.

items.title: Billing & Revenue Recovery
items.description: Automate invoice generation, process batches by basin or cycle and track payments, and outstanding balances in one place.
items.imageAlt: SIRH billing and revenue recovery module

ecosystem.title:
Scalable ecosystem:
the evolution of your digital strategy
ecosystem.description:
SIRH is designed to grow alongside your institution.
Once core management is consolidated, the system can expand through advanced tools to further digitalise field operations and public transparency.

modules.title: SIRH Mobile
modules.description: A dedicated application for field teams to collect, update, and validate data on-site.
modules.imageAlt: SIRH Mobile mockup showing field map editing with GPS coordinates

features.label: Paperless Fieldwork
features.text: Eliminate manual forms and reduce errors by entering data directly into the system.

features.label: Real-time Reliability
features.text: Information collected in the field is synchronised with the central registry.

features.label: Improved Quality
features.text: High-consistency data collection including precise GPS coordinates.

modules.title: Digital Customer Portal
modules.description: A digital platform to improve communication and transparency with water users.
modules.imageAlt: Customer portal mockup on tablet and smartphone showing operation areas and licenses

features.label: License & Billing Information
features.text: Water users can access up-to-date details of their licenses, invoices, balances, and key dates.

features.label: Licensing Interactions
features.text: Users can submit applications, upload supporting documents, and respond to requests from the institution.

features.label: Status Tracking
features.text: Both users and the institution track each licensing step in one shared workflow.

methodology.title:
Our difference: collaborative implementation
that leads to technology ownership
methodology.description: We don't just deliver software—you build it with us.
methodology.imageAlt: Workshop reviewing printed water licenses alongside the digital system

items.title: Multidisciplinary Co-design
items.description: We establish cross-institutional working groups to design the system alongside the end-users, ensuring it fits your specific workflows and needs.

items.title: Phased Growth Strategy
items.description: Implementation is structured in three strategic phases (Water User and License Registry, Procedures, and Billing) to reduce risk and allow progressive institutional adaptation.

items.title: Institutional Strengthening
items.description: We dedicate 40-50% of the project effort to co-design workshops, training and capacity building.

items.title: Open-Source Philosophy
items.description: Total ownership of code and data by the institution, eliminating vendor lock-in and high licensing costs.

contact.title: Ready to modernise your water administration?
contact.description: Contact our team for technical questions, budget estimations or a platform demo.

footer.tagline: We support water authorities overcoming technological constraints.
